creator: olivier@cochard.me
text:
Hi,

I meet a regression since 8.0 Beta1 to 8.0 RC1.

They can't detect my PATA (UDMA 100) hard drive (which works great under FreeBSD 7.2).

During bootup, I've got lot's of theses error messages:

ad0: FAILURE - READ_DMA48 timed out LBA=586072366
ad0: TIMEOUT - READ_DMA retrying (1 retries left) LBA=0
ad0: FAILURE - READ_DMA48 timed out LBA=586072351
ad0: TIMEOUT - READ_DMA retrying (0 retries left) LBA=0
ad0: FAILURE - READ_DMA48 timed out LBA=586072351
(...)
ad0: FAILURE - READ_DMA48 status=51 <READY,DSC,ERROR> error=10<NID_NOT_FOUND> LBA=596072367
ad0: FAILURE - READ_DMA48 timed out LBA=586072366
ad0: TIMEOUT - READ_DMA retrying (1 retries left) LBA=128
ad0: TIMEOUT - READ_DMA retrying (0 retries left) LBA=128
ad0: FAILURE - READ_DMA48 timed out LBA=586072351
(...)
This scan step takes a long times (about 5 minutes), then it continue to boot, but without detecting the hard drive.

My system use an Asus K8N4-E Delux motherboard  (nVidia nForce CK804 UDMA133 controller).

You can found on the attached file, the dmesge and pcinfo -lv from my working FreeBSD 7.2.

Thanks,

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: Booting the system with FreeBSD 8.0 RC1 memstick release.
